name: Grandma Oakes Chicken and Dumplings
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix the dry ingredients in a bowl.
2. Add the shortening and ice water, stirring until combined. If needed, add a little more flour to achieve a smooth batter.
3. Roll out the batter on a floured surface very thinly.
4. Cut into strips measuring approximately 1 inch by 3 inches.
5. Cut up one chicken and place it in a quart-sized pot of water. Add salt and pepper, as well as half an onion.
6. Bring to a boil until the chicken is cooked through (you may choose to debone the chicken, though Grandma Oakes never did).
7. Gently place dumplings on top of the broth.
8. Slowly simmer the pot, stirring occasionally with a fork in the juice.
9. Gradually heat 1 cup of milk and slowly pour it into the pot.
10. Cook for approximately 45 minutes or until the dumplings are done.
